text: The laparoscopic camera is introduced into the abdominal cavity, providing a view of the internal structures as the surgeon begins exploration.
The surgeon places a 5mm port in the left lower quadrant and another 5mm port in the suprapubic area, preparing for the appendectomy procedure.
The surgeon reveals and identifies the appendix using ligature, then cuts the mass of the appendix and releases it from surrounding tissue.
The surgeon erases the radix of the appendix, completing the removal of the appendix from its base.
The surgeon uses an end-loop with a number zero V-create to ligate the appendix twice at the top, completing the appendectomy with visible ligatures securing the structure.
The surgeon removes the appendix through the ten-millimeter camera port, completing the extraction process.

The surgeon explores the abdominal cavity and small intestine using a camera, detecting a Meckel's diverticulum in the terminal ileum. A five-millimeter port in the bottom left quadrant is removed and replaced with a twelve-millimeter port at the same incision site.
The surgeon excises the Meckel's diverticulum using a linear laparoscopic stapler, completing the removal of the diverticulum.
The excised Meckel's diverticulum is removed from the abdominal cavity through the twelve-millimeter port in the left lower quadrant.
